Skip to content Upwork home Toggle Search Use arrow keys to navigate between search suggestions Search Clear Input Filters Advanced search Save search Save this search Saved jobs Saved jobs 0 Posted 9 minutes ago Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) Expert & UX Auditor (Luxury Brand) More options Save Job Verified Payment verified Rating is 0 out of 5. 0 $9K+ spent Location United States Hourly: $10.00 - $20.00 Expert Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week The Million Roses is undergoing a strategic rebranding and repositioning to an ultra-luxury brand. We need someone who deeply understands luxury brands and wealthy consumer behavior to revise our entire website and user journey. We need a partner who finds every friction point, mistake, and drop-off to uncover opportunities to boost conversions. We are not interested in mass-market e-commerce tactics like adding money-back guarantees, promo banners, or discounts etc. Furthermore, we are not looking for someone who will simply run a Google PageSpeed Insights test and submit an automated export. We need a website that sells, converts, and justifies our premium prices to boost sales. Our core objective is to acquire high-net-worth individuals who purchase our products at full price without hesitation. You will identify the bugs and friction points and provide a thorough list of actionable insights and items that will help us to boost our CVR. Our internal development team will handle all technical implementation. You will analyze GA4 data, user session recordings, and heatmaps etc to understand exactly how high-net-worth individuals navigate our site. We require an expert who can find opportunities to boost conversion rates through elegance and flawless user experience. Core objectives: Conduct a comprehensive website audit of the entire digital user journey to identify friction points, technical/website bugs and drop-off zones. Compile a thorough, prioritized list of all identified issues and opportunities for our internal development team to implement and also how other ultra-luxury brands are doing that we can incorporate. Analyze GA4 data, user session recordings, and heatmaps to extract behavioral insights. Provide actionable insights, quick wins to improve conversion rates and revenue potential. Elevate the perceived value of the brand through strategic layout, flow, and user experience adjustments that justify our premium pricing. Establish the foundation for a long-term partnership based on proven, data-backed revenue growth. Requirements: Proven track record of optimizing digital experiences for luxury brands targeting high-net-worth individuals. Deep understanding of luxury consumer psychology and how to build digital desire without relying on price incentives. Expertise in advanced data analysis. Ability to identify subtle technical bugs and UX flaws that degrade a premium experience. A meticulous eye for detail and a strategic mindset focused on long-term brand equity. Brand Positioning Conversion Rate Optimization Google Analytics A/B Testing Marketing Strategy User Experience Design +3 Posted 11 minutes ago Lead Generation Specialist Needed (Google Maps Data Extraction) More options Save Job Unverified Payment unverified Rating is 0 out of 5. 0 $0 spent Location Pakistan Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $50.00 Hi, I’m looking for someone reliable and detail-oriented to help me collect business leads from Google Maps. This is a simple but important task, so accuracy and attention to detail really matter. Find businesses on Google Maps based on given niches and locations Collect details like: Business Name Address Phone Number Website Email (if available) Make sure there are no duplicates Organize everything neatly in Excel or Google Sheets What I’m looking for: Someone with experience in lead generation or web research Good attention to detail Able to deliver clean and accurate data Good communication and responsiveness Project Details: Location: [e.g., Quebec / Canada] Niches: Restaurants, Hotels, Clinics, Spas, etc. Please share a bit about your experience and any similar work you’ve done. Also, write “TRACK” at the start of your proposal so I know you’ve read the full job description 🙂 Data Scraping Data Mining Data Entry Lead Generation List Building Prospect List Proposals: Less than 5 Posted 1 hour ago Short-Term Rental PMS Migration – Message & Automation Extraction (Hospitable → excel/Google Sheet) More options Save Job Verified Payment verified Rating is 4.3 out of 5. 4.3 $1K+ spent Location United States Hourly Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week I am currently transitioning my short-term rental properties from Hospitable to another PMS, and I’m looking for someone to help extract and organize all existing messages and automations. Scope of work: You will extract all messages (automated and canned) from Hospitable and organize them into a structured Google Sheet. Each message should be entered as one row, including: • Message Name • Type (Automated / Canned) • Category (Stay / Info / Upsells / Issues) • Stage (Inquiry / Booking / Pre-arrival / Stay / Checkout / Post-stay) • Trigger (written clearly, e.g. “Check-in → 2 days before”) • Property • Full message text (complete content, no rewriting) Important: • This is an extraction and organization task only — no rewriting or editing of the messages • All variables/parameters must be kept exactly as they appear (e.g. listing_area) • Please add a “Notes” column to include any suggestions for structure, simplification, or missing messages (without modifying the original content) • If you identify missing messages (e.g. deposit, rental agreement, key steps), please add them as separate lines and note them in the “Notes” column Process: We will start with one property to validate the format before proceeding with the full project. What I’m looking for: • Experience with PMS tools (Hospitable experience is a plus) • Strong attention to detail • Ability to organize information clearly and logically • Clear communication To apply: Please briefly explain your experience with similar projects and how you would approach this task. Looking forward to working together, Bénédicte Please include the word ‘structure’ in your reply so I know you’ve read this. Automation Microsoft Excel Google Sheets Customer Support Property Management Software Proposals: 5 to 10 Posted 1 hour ago Data Processing + Google Sheets Specialist More options Save Job Verified Payment verified Rating is 5.0 out of 5. 5.0 $20K+ spent Location USA Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $85.00 Overview We’re looking for a detail-oriented freelancer to help build a clean, structured dataset and Google Sheets workflow from provided data sources. This is a one-time project focused on: Data cleaning Standardization Structured formatting Building a usable system for internal workflows Accuracy and consistency are critical. Scope of Work 1. Process Provided Dataset (CSV) We will provide a CSV file containing structured and semi-structured profile data. Your responsibilities: Clean and standardize all fields Remove duplicates Normalize formatting (names, text fields, etc.) Handle missing or inconsistent data Map fields into a defined Google Sheets structure No data sourcing is required. 2. Compile Additional Publicly Available Data We will provide a list of publicly accessible sources. Your role is to: Compile relevant data points from these sources Structure the data into consistent fields Ensure quality and completeness All work should rely only on publicly available information and standard tools. 3. Build the Master Google Sheet You will create a structured Google Sheet with: Clearly defined columns Consistent formatting across all rows Dropdown fields for status tracking Clean layout for daily internal use The sheet must be easy to use and free of inconsistencies. Deliverables Cleaned and standardized dataset loaded into Google Sheets Fully structured sheet with consistent formatting Test batch (small sample) before full delivery Brief Loom walkthrough (5–10 minutes) explaining the setup Important Requirements High attention to detail Consistent formatting across all data Ability to work with large datasets Clear communication Preferred Experience Data cleaning and processing Google Sheets (advanced usage) Working with CSV files and structured datasets Experience using data tools or automation workflows How to Apply Please include: A brief example of a similar data processing project Your approach to ensuring data consistency and accuracy Your timeline Your proposed fixed price Compensation Fixed price project Paid test batch before full work begins Final Note We’re looking for someone who can deliver clean, reliable, well-structured data that can be used immediately without additional cleanup. Data Analysis Proposals: 10 to 15 Posted 2 hours ago Hillsborough County Florida Scraper More options Save Job Verified Payment verified Rating is 5.0 out of 5. 5.0 $300+ spent Location United States Fixed price Intermediate Est. budget: $100.00 https://webapps.hcso.tampa.fl.us/arrestinquiry I need someone to develop a scraper to obtain names, addresses, dates of birth, charge description, of all people arrested on a daily basis from the Hillsborough County sheriff's office in Hillsborough County, Tampa Florida. See link above I need the names in an excel sheet where I can put them into a mail merge word document and mail off advertisement letters. I already have this same set up in another county, so I will be able to show whoever takes this task exactly what I want and need. I have already purchased captchas, but I may need help linking the captchas with the program once it is built. See attached sample of excel format I want. https://webapps.hcso.tampa.fl.us/ArrestInquiry Data Scraping Proposals: 20 to 50 Posted 2 hours ago LLM + Vision Resume Parsing, Taxonomy & Knowledge Graph (High Accuracy Required) More options Save Job Verified Payment verified Rating is 4.9 out of 5. 4.9 $200K+ spent Location United States Hourly: $30.00 - $60.00 Expert Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/week We are building a next-generation AI-powered resume parsing and enrichment platform for the staffing industry. We have already attempted a solution but are not achieving acceptable accuracy in employment history extraction (company names, roles, timelines). We are looking for a hands-on expert who has successfully built or significantly improved resume/CV parsers using LLMs + document vision models, and can architect a robust, production-grade pipeline. This is not a basic parsing task. The goal is high-precision structured data extraction + entity normalization + taxonomy mapping at scale. Core Objectives 1. Resume Parsing (LLM + Vision Hybrid) Extract structured data from resumes (PDF, DOCX, scanned formats): Employment history (company, title, start/end dates) Education (school, degree, dates) Skills Solve real-world issues: Inconsistent formatting Missing/implicit company names Multi-column resumes OCR noise (for scanned resumes) Improve accuracy specifically for: Company name extraction (critical gap today) Timeline continuity and role segmentation 2. Company Normalization + Industry Mapping Build a company taxonomy + normalization system: Resolve variations (e.g., “IBM”, “International Business Machines”) Deduplicate entities Map companies to industries using: NAICS codes (or equivalent like SIC) Public datasets/APIs (OpenCorporates, Clearbit, Crunchbase, etc.) Output: Canonical company name Industry classification Confidence score 3. Skills Taxonomy + Graph Mapping Build a skills ontology/taxonomy: Normalize variants (e.g., “JS” → JavaScript) Group into clusters (Frontend, Backend, AI, DevOps, etc.) Create a skills graph: Relationships (e.g., React → JavaScript → Frontend) Transferability / adjacency mapping Map each candidate to: Standardized skill set Skill clust...[truncated]
